name: Pastéis de Belém
notes: Original recipe since 1837, made by monks at Jerónimos Monastery. The most famous pastel de nata shop in Portugal.

name: Manteigaria - Chiado
notes: €1.50 per tart, exclusive focus on perfecting pastéis de nata. Art Nouveau façade from 1900. Open 8am-midnight.

name: Manteigaria - Time Out Market
notes: Time Out Market location in Cais do Sodré. Same quality as Chiado location.

name: Manteigaria - Rua Augusta
notes: City center Baixa location on pedestrian shopping street. Convenient for tourists.

name: Pastelaria Aloma - Campo de Ourique
notes: Four-time National Contest winner (2012, 2013, 2015, 2024). Open daily 8am-7pm.

name: Confeitaria Nacional
notes: Since 1829. Recipe nearly 200 years old from founder Balthazar Castanheiro. Near Rossio.

name: Pastelaria Santo António - Alfama
notes: Won 'Best Pastel de Nata in Lisbon' 2019. Near São Jorge Castle in beautiful tile-covered building.

name: Pastelaria Santo António - Chiado
notes: Second location opened 2025 in downtown Chiado. Award-winning quality.

name: Aloma - Lisbon Airport
notes: Airport location in Terminal 1. Get your natas before you fly!

name: Manteigaria - Belém
notes: Belém location near Pastéis de Belém. Fresh natas all day.

name: Nata de Lisboa
notes: Smooth filling, perfect sweetness, crispy crust. Popular downtown location.

name: Fábrica da Nata - Praça dos Restauradores
notes: Flagship location. Multiple shops across city. Fresh natas all day.

name: Fábrica da Nata - Rua Augusta
notes: Downtown pedestrian street location. Great for tourists.

name: Castro - Chiado
notes: Opened 2017 by Chef André Castro. Chiado neighborhood. High quality artisan natas.

name: Nat'elier
notes: Run by chef João Batalha. Offers workshops. Downtown Lisbon.

name: Vegan Nata Chiado
notes: Plant-based pastel de nata. Chiado location. Vegan-friendly.

name: Pastelaria Versailles
notes: Since 1922. Belle Époque style interior. Traditional Portuguese café.

name: Landeau Chocolate
notes: Famous for chocolate cake but also serves excellent natas. Chiado.

name: Padaria Ribeiro
notes: Traditional neighborhood bakery. Local favorite since 1920s.

name: Pastelaria Cristal
notes: Family-run since 1940. Saldanha neighborhood. Authentic local spot.

name: Briosa
notes: Gourmet Portuguese pastries. Elegant Chiado location.

name: A Padaria Portuguesa - Chiado
notes: Modern bakery chain. Fresh daily. Multiple locations.

name: Casa Suíça
notes: Since 1922. Historic Rossio square location. Traditional café.

name: Pastelaria Santo Amaro
notes: Family bakery. Cais do Sodré area. Local neighborhood gem.

name: Fábrica da Nata - Colombo
notes: Colombo Shopping Centre location. Convenient for shoppers.
